findings.title: Potential secret detected: no-hardcoded-secret-unquoted
findings.description: A pattern matching 'no-hardcoded-secret-unquoted' was found. This could expose sensitive credentials.
findings.recommendation: Move this value to an environment variable or a secrets manager.

findings.title: Potential secret detected: no-connection-string
findings.description: A pattern matching 'no-connection-string' was found. This could expose sensitive credentials.
findings.recommendation: Move this value to an environment variable or a secrets manager.

findings.title: All requests permitted without authentication
findings.description: Spring Security is configured with anyRequest().permitAll(), effectively disabling authentication for all endpoints. Any client can access any endpoint without credentials.
findings.recommendation: Replace .anyRequest().permitAll() with specific matchers: e.g. .requestMatchers("/public/**").permitAll().anyRequest().authenticated()

findings.title: CSRF protection is disabled
findings.description: Spring Security CSRF protection is explicitly disabled. This exposes the application to cross-site request forgery attacks unless the API is purely stateless with token-based auth.
findings.recommendation: If the API uses session-based auth or cookies, re-enable CSRF. If purely stateless with JWT/Bearer tokens and no cookies, CSRF disable may be acceptable — add a comment documenting the rationale.

findings.title: CORS allows wildcard origin with credentials
findings.description: CORS is configured to allow origin '*' together with allowCredentials(true). Modern browsers block this combination, but misconfigured proxies or older clients may still honor it, enabling credential theft via cross-origin requests.
findings.recommendation: Replace the wildcard origin with an explicit allowlist of trusted origins. Use an environment variable to configure allowed origins per environment.

findings.title: Missing @Valid on @RequestBody parameter
findings.description: A @RequestBody parameter lacks @Valid or @Validated annotation. Without it, Bean Validation constraints on the DTO are silently ignored, allowing malformed input to reach the service layer.
findings.recommendation: Add @Valid before @RequestBody: public ResponseEntity<?> create(@Valid @RequestBody MyDTO dto)

findings.title: Exception message leaked to client
findings.description: An @ExceptionHandler returns e.getMessage() or e.toString() directly to the client. Internal exceptions (NullPointerException, JDBC errors, etc.) expose implementation details such as table names, stack info, and database error messages.
findings.recommendation: Return a generic error message to clients (e.g., "An internal error occurred"). Log the full exception server-side for debugging.

findings.title: All Actuator endpoints exposed
findings.description: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=* exposes all Spring Boot Actuator endpoints, including sensitive ones like /env, /configprops, /heapdump, and /shutdown. Without authentication, this is an information leak.
findings.recommendation: Limit exposure to safe endpoints: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,prometheus. If broader exposure is needed, ensure Spring Security protects the management port.

findings.title: Layer violation: presentation → domain
findings.description: File in 'presentation' layer imports from 'domain' layer. Dependencies should flow inward (presentation → application → domain ← infrastructure).
findings.recommendation: Invert the dependency using an interface in the domain layer.

findings.title: TODO comment: add pagination
findings.recommendation: Resolve the TODO/FIXME or create a tracked issue.

findings.title: FIXME comment: this self-injection is a workaround for @Transactional proxy

evidence.snippet: // FIXME: this self-injection is a workaround for @Transactional proxy

findings.recommendation: Resolve the TODO/FIXME or create a tracked issue.

findings.title: TODO comment: implement order processing logic
findings.recommendation: Resolve the TODO/FIXME or create a tracked issue.

findings.title: README.md is missing
findings.description: Project has no root README, which makes onboarding and usage harder.
findings.recommendation: Add a README with setup, usage, and testing instructions.

findings.title: No ADR documents found
findings.description: No architecture decision records detected in docs/adr or docs/adrs.
findings.recommendation: Add ADRs for major architecture and technology decisions.

findings.title: Deep nesting (5 levels) in src\main\java\com\example\service\UserService.java
findings.description: src\main\java\com\example\service\UserService.java has code nested 5 levels deep. Deep nesting makes code hard to follow and test.
findings.recommendation: Flatten nesting with early returns (guard clauses), extract inner blocks into named functions, or use strategy/command patterns to eliminate deep conditionals.

findings.title: @Lazy self-injection (circular dependency): src\main\java\com\example\service\OrderService.java
findings.description: @Lazy is used to break a circular dependency at injection time. This is a code smell indicating the classes have a bidirectional dependency that should be resolved through extraction of shared logic into a separate service.
findings.recommendation: Extract the shared logic into a dedicated service to break the cycle. Alternatively, use event-driven communication or the mediator pattern.

findings.title: Missing @Modifying on mutating @Query: src\main\java\com\example\repository\UserRepository.java
findings.description: A @Query with UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT requires @Modifying annotation. Without it, Spring Data JPA treats it as a SELECT, causing a runtime exception.

evidence.snippet: @Query("UPDATE User u SET u.active = false WHERE u.loginAttempts > 5")

findings.recommendation: Add @Modifying (and optionally @Transactional) above the @Query annotation.

findings.title: No @Version for optimistic locking: src\main\java\com\example\entity\Department.java
findings.description: This JPA entity has no @Version field. Without optimistic locking, concurrent updates silently overwrite each other (lost update problem).
findings.recommendation: Add a @Version field: @Version private Long version; JPA will automatically throw OptimisticLockException on conflicting updates.

findings.title: @Data on JPA entity: src\main\java\com\example\entity\User.java
findings.description: @Data (Lombok) on a JPA @Entity generates equals(), hashCode(), and toString() on ALL fields, including lazy-loaded relationships. This triggers unintended lazy loading, LazyInitializationException outside sessions, and StackOverflowError on bidirectional toString().
findings.recommendation: Replace @Data with @Getter and @Setter. Implement equals()/hashCode() manually using business keys or entity ID only. Exclude lazy fields from toString().

findings.title: CascadeType.ALL on @ManyToOne: src\main\java\com\example\entity\User.java
findings.description: CascadeType.ALL on a @ManyToOne relationship means child operations cascade to the parent. Deleting or modifying the child can unintentionally delete/modify the parent entity.
findings.recommendation: Remove CascadeType.ALL from @ManyToOne. Use specific cascades (PERSIST, MERGE) only on @OneToMany (parent → children) where it makes semantic sense.

findings.title: No @Version for optimistic locking: src\main\java\com\example\entity\User.java
findings.description: This JPA entity has no @Version field. Without optimistic locking, concurrent updates silently overwrite each other (lost update problem).
findings.recommendation: Add a @Version field: @Version private Long version; JPA will automatically throw OptimisticLockException on conflicting updates.

findings.title: JPA entities found but no database migration tool detected
findings.description: The project uses JPA @Entity classes but neither Liquibase nor Flyway is configured. Without a migration tool, schema changes are untracked, making rollbacks and team collaboration risky. Relying on hibernate.ddl-auto=update in production leads to data loss.
findings.recommendation: Add Liquibase or Flyway to your build (e.g. spring-boot-starter-liquibase). Create an initial changelog from your current schema, then track all DDL changes as versioned migrations.

findings.title: HttpSession used in REST controller: src\main\java\com\example\controller\UserController.java
findings.description: A REST controller injects HttpSession or calls getSession(). REST APIs should be stateless — using server-side sessions breaks horizontal scalability and requires sticky sessions for load balancing.
findings.recommendation: Replace session usage with stateless authentication (JWT, OAuth2 tokens). Store user context in the token payload, not the server session.

findings.title: Unpaginated list endpoint: src\main\java\com\example\controller\UserController.java
findings.description: A controller returns List/Collection without pagination (no Pageable parameter or Page return type). With growing data, this causes memory spikes and slow responses.
findings.recommendation: Accept a Pageable parameter and return Page<T> instead of List<T>. Example: Page<UserDTO> getUsers(Pageable pageable)

findings.title: 1 catch block(s) swallow exceptions without logging
findings.description: Found 1 catch block(s) that neither log nor re-throw the caught exception. Silent failures prevent operators from diagnosing production issues.
findings.recommendation: Add at least logger.error(err) or logger.warn(err) inside every catch block, or re-throw after logging.

findings.title: No distributed tracing setup detected
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, Jaeger, or Zipkin tracing initialization was found. Distributed tracing is essential for diagnosing latency issues in multi-service architectures.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with OpenTelemetry: npm install @opentelemetry/sdk-node @opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node and create a tracing.ts bootstrap file.

root_causes_reported.title: SRP violations and maintainability hotspots are accumulating
Code quality guardrails are not enforced
Documentation is not maintained with code changes
REST API conventions are not standardized
